item.title: ECB’s Kaasik Says One More Rate Hike Is Reasonable Expectation
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Ulo Kaasik said further moentary tightening may be needed to ensure inflation returns to target after the Iran war.

item.title: Krafton Agrees to Pay ‘Subnautica 2’ Bonuses as Developer’s CEO Resigns
item.description: After being fired over a $250 million payout dispute and then reinstated, Unknown Worlds Entertainment boss Ted Gill is leaving again

item.title: Bank of England’s Bailey Says Rate Cuts Are Still Off the Table
item.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey cautioned that it is too early to consider interest-rate cuts, warning households are yet to feel the full effect of the Iran war.

item.title: Peru’s Inflation Unexpectedly Picks Up With Rising Food Prices
item.description: Inflation in Peru’s capital unexpectedly accelerated in June, staying above the central bank’s target range for a fourth straight month as rising food costs add to the recent global energy shock.

item.title: Uber Shakes Up AI Data Labeling Business, Dismissing Top Leaders
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. has dismissed two tech leaders at its nascent AI data labeling business, shaking up a key division that the ride-hail company has positioned as a key growth driver.

item.title: Pimco Warns Private Credit ‘Confidence Gap’ to Reveal Weak Funds
item.description: Private credit investors have become increasingly discerning about dispersion in investment marks, creating a “confidence gap” between asset managers, according to Pacific Investment Management Co.

item.title: US Manufacturing Expands at Slower Pace, Costs Gauge Drops
item.description: US manufacturing activity expanded for a sixth straight month in June as a war-driven surge in input costs eased.

item.title: Milei Tries Again to Revive Argentina’s Dormant Mortgage Market
item.description: President Javier Milei is trying to tackle Argentina’s longstanding lack of home mortgages in a bid to turn around a construction sector that’s losing jobs before his reelection bid next year.

item.title: Lagarde Says Inflation, Growth Risks Now More Broadly Balanced
item.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said risks to euro-area inflation and growth have become less pronounced.

item.title: Warsh Says Inflation Risks Are Down, Vows Price Stability
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh said price risks have come down in recent weeks, while repeating his determination to bring inflation back to the US central bank’s 2% target.

item.title: Sony to End Production of Physical PlayStation Discs in 2028
item.description: Sony Group Corp. will end production of physical discs for its PlayStation video-game consoles starting in January 2028, going all in on a digital approach that will require consumers to download titles from the company’s online storefront moving forward.

item.title: US Limits on Anthropic’s Mythos Keep Foreign Firms in Limbo
item.description: Foreign governments, companies and financial institutions were left in limbo after the Trump administration continued to restrict access to Anthropic PBC’s Mythos 5 to a limited number of US organizations even after dropping export controls on the company’s latest artificial intelligence models.

item.title: US Stocks Fall as Traders Evaluate Warsh Comments, New Data
item.description: US stocks pared earlier losses as fresh economic data painted a picture of resilience, while traders assessed commentary from Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh.

item.title: Meta Is Building a Cloud Business to Sell Excess AI Compute
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is developing plans for a cloud infrastructure business that will sell access to AI computing power and models, setting up a new vector of competition with industry leaders like Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud.

item.title: Fertilizer Firm Draws $12 Billion In Orders in Oman IPO
item.description: Oman India Fertiliser Co.’s initial public offering has drawn over $12 billion in orders, in the Middle East’s largest listing since the outbreak of the regional conflict.

item.title: S&P Winning Streak for July at Risk With Volatile End to Month
item.description: July is a great time to buy stocks. In fact, it’s been the best month for the S&P 500 Index in the past two decades. Bulls are finding comfort in that history ahead of what stands to be an eventful stretch.

item.title: Software Gains as Guggenheim Says AI No ‘Death Knell’ for Sector
item.description: Software shares rose in premarket trading after Guggenheim upgraded a trio of companies, saying fears that artificial intelligence poses a mortal threat to the sector are overdone.

item.title: US Companies Added 98,000 Jobs in June, ADP Data Show
item.description: US private-sector job creation was solid again in June, capping the best three-month stretch for hiring in more than a year.

item.title: Coffee, Sugar Extend Rally as Adverse Weather Fuels Supply Fears
item.description: Coffee and sugar futures extended gains in New York and London as unfavorable weather in key growing regions heightened supply concerns.

item.title: Taiwan Detains Super Micro Workers in China Chip Smuggling Probe
item.description: Taiwanese prosecutors detained two Super Micro Computer Inc. employees following a raid of the US company’s local offices earlier this week over alleged shipments of Nvidia Corp. chips to China.

item.title: Fed’s Warsh, ECB’s Lagarde Speak at Sintra Conference
item.description: ECB President Christine Lagarde and Kevin Warsh Photographer: Claus Dirk/ECB

item.title: Google Told to Pay Klarna Nearly $2 Billion in Shopping Spat
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google was ordered to pay almost $2 billion to Klarna Group Plc’s Pricerunner unit in a dispute over the search-engine giant’s abuse of power in the market for comparison shopping services.

item.title: Commodities Broker Pulled Back Into Tax Dividend Fraud Suit by UK Court
item.description: ED&F Man Capital Markets Ltd., now MCML Ltd., has been pulled back into a long-running fraud case linked to the Cum-Ex tax dividend scandal after the UK Supreme Court ruled that the Danish tax authority’s lawsuit against the commodities broker can proceed.

item.title: Trump’s White House Takes Aim at EU Emissions Law
item.description: With the US warning it could ship its energy elsewhere, some countries in Europe are urging the bloc to revisit the rules.

item.title: Emerging Market Currencies Erase 2026 Gains as Dollar Advances
item.description: Emerging-market currencies erased gains for this year, with speculation over higher US interest rates sparking a resurgence in the US dollar.

item.title: Bending Spoons IPO Turns Italian College Pals Into Billionaires
item.description: Four former college friends who founded software rollup Bending Spoons SpA have emerged as Italy’s youngest cohort of self-made billionaires.

item.title: JPMorgan Is Leading Race for French Cooling Network Provider Idex
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s asset management unit has emerged as the leading bidder to buy Antin Infrastructure Partners SA’s French heating and cooling network operator, Idex, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Greece’s Jobless Rate Falls to 8.1% in May, Lowest Since 2008
item.description: Greece’s seasonally-adjusted unemployment rate fell to 8.1% in May, the lowest level since November 2008, according to the Hellenic Statistical Authority. That compares with a revised reading of 9.1% in April.

item.title: Oracle Warns Its Splurge on AI Data Centers May Not Pay Off
item.description: The company outlines the risks of its massive cloud build-out to meet AI demand

item.title: AI Productivity Hopes Collide With Job and Market Worries
item.description: OpenAI’s chief economist has soothing words on AI’s employment impact, but job cuts are happening.
